body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Google Secretary rack device
body.description:
**What changed:** GoogleSecretaryDevice added to the rack — a device that holds real credentials and sends real emails/calendar events on behalf of Igor's account.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Credential leakage: shim config ends up in git if developer forgets gitignore pattern
- Spam/rate-limit: integration tests run in CI with live credentials and trigger Gmail rate limits
- Authorization creep: other devices import secretary tools directly instead of going via bus, bypassing the dispatcher's escalate path
- Identity confusion: emails sent 'from Igor' don't clearly identify as automated, causing Akien confusion about who sent what

**Signals to watch:**
- `git log --all -- '*.json' '*.yaml' | grep -i cred` — credential files in history
- Gmail send rate in Google Cloud console
- Channel posts showing direct tool imports outside bus path
- Email headers — is 'from' clearly labeled as automated?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15 or when T-google-secretary-email-test closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Observation ticket — done when each predicted effect is annotated as occurred / clear / partial
